title: Preprocessor operators # and ## should be avoided
description: Preprocessor operators "#" and "##" should not be usedAnalyze your codeintentionality-clearmaintainabilityCode Smellbased-on-misralock-inWhy is this an issue?More InfoThe evaluation order of both the#and##preprocessor operators is unspecified. Compilers have been known to implement these operators inconsistently, therefore, to avoid these problems, do not use them.Noncompliant code example#define A(Y) #Y /* Noncompliant */ #define A(X,Y) X##Y /* Noncompliant */Available In:Catch issues on the fly,in your IDEDetect issues in your GitHub, Azure DevOps Services, Bitbucket Cloud, GitLab repositoriesAnalyze code in youron-premise CIDeveloper EditionAvailable Since9.1©2025SonarSource Sàrl. recommendation: Refactor to eliminate the flagged pattern.

checks.message: Preprocessor operators # and ## should be avoided - use explicit alternatives
